zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert d'Eu Lord of Hastings Comte d'Eu於1010年出生在法國諾門迪瑟-馬利艾時，他的父親Guillaume comte d'Eu et d'Hiémois30歲，母親Lasseline De Harcourt35歲。他於大約1077年與Mathilde de Sicily在西西利亞卡坦尼亞結婚。他於1066年登記服兵役。他去世於1093年9月8日在英國素可海斯廷斯享年83歲，埋葬在法國諾門迪瑟-馬利勒 特雷波特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert d'Eu Lord of Hastings Comte d'Eu于1010年出生在法国諾門迪瑟-馬利艾时，他的父亲Guillaume comte d'Eu et d'Hiémois30岁，母亲Lasseline De Harcourt35岁。他于大约1077年与Mathilde de Sicily在西西利亞卡坦尼亞结婚。他于1066年登记服兵役。他去世于1093年9月8日在英国素可海斯廷斯享年83岁，埋葬在法国諾門迪瑟-馬利勒 特雷波特。

nameMeanings.name: d'Eu
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

variants.name: De
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Day
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English day(e), dey(e) ‘dairyman or dairymaid’. Originally used only of women, it was later used of men with the sense ‘man in charge of the dairy cattle’. This is probably the most common source of the surname.

English: from the Middle English personal name Day(e) or Dey. In western England this is probably a pet form of David , but in northern England and perhaps elsewhere also it is a late Middle English variant of Daw, a pet form of Ralph (see Daw , Dakin ).
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Deaghaidh (see O'Dea ).

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Sweyn Forkbeard
historicEvents.description: The Danes raided and captured England. Sweyn, King of Danes, was declared King of England on Christmas Day.

historicEvents.title: Canut the Great
historicEvents.description: As Aethelred, King of England died, his son, Edmund Ironside devised a truce with Danish King Canute. The truce divided the kingdom between the two but Edmund died shortly after and became King of England. Several years later, he also became king of Denmark.

historicEvents.title: England Divided
historicEvents.description: King Canute died in 1035 and England was divided between his three sons. His son Sweyn received Norway, Harthacanute received Denmark, and Harold Harefoot received North England.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert d'Eu Lord of Hastings Comte d'Eu was born in 1010, in Eu, Seine-Maritime, Normandy, France, his father, Guillaume comte d'Eu et d'Hiémois, was 30 and his mother, Lasseline De Harcourt, was 35. He married Mathilde de Sicily about 1077, in Catania, Sicily. He registered for military service in 1066. He died on 8 September 1093, in Hastings, Sussex, England, at the age of 83, and was buried in Le Tréport, Seine-Maritime, Normandy, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert d'Eu Lord of Hastings Comte d'Eu est né en 1010, à Eu, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, France, son père, Guillaume comte d'Eu et d'Hiémois, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Lasseline De Harcourt, avait 35 ans. Il a épousé Mathilde de Sicily environ 1077, à Catania, Sicile. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1066. Il est décédé le 8 septembre 1093, à Hastings, Sussex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 83 ans, et a été enterré à Le Tréport, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert d'Eu Lord of Hastings Comte d'Eu 1010 in Eu, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Guillaume comte d'Eu et d'Hiémois 30 und seine Mutter Lasseline De Harcourt 35. Er hat um 1077 in Catania, Sizilien Mathilde de Sicily geheiratet. Er hat sich 1066 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb am 8. September 1093 im Alter von 83 Jahren in Hastings, Sussex, England und wurde in Le Tréport, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert d'Eu Lord of Hastings Comte d'Eu è nato il nel 1010 nel Eu, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia, il suo padre, Guillaume comte d'Eu et d'Hiémois, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Lasseline De Harcourt, aveva 35 anni. Lui sposato Mathilde de Sicily circa 1077, nel Catania, Sicilia. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1066. Morì il 8 settembre 1093, nel Hastings, Sussex, England, all'età di 83 anni, e fu sepolto a Le Tréport, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert d'Eu Lord of Hastings Comte d'Euが1010年にフランスノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメエウで生まれた時，父Guillaume comte d'Eu et d'Hiémoisは30歳，母Lasseline De Harcourtは35歳であった。約1077年にシシリアカタニアでMathilde de Sicilyと結婚した。彼は1066年に兵役登録をした。彼は1093年9月8日にイギリススセックスハスティングズで83歳で死去し，フランスノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメル トルeプオルトにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert d'Eu Lord of Hastings Comte d'Eu(이)가 1010년 프랑스 노먼디 세이인-마이팀 우에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Guillaume comte d'Eu et d'Hiémois(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Lasseline De Harcourt(은)는 35세였다. 그는 대략 1077년 시실리야 카탠이아에서 Mathilde de Sicily(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1066년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1093년 9월 8일 영국 수세스 하스틴그스에서 83세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 노먼디 세이인-마이팀 일 트e포트에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert d'Eu Lord of Hastings Comte d'Eu nasceu em 1010, em Eu, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, França, seu pai, Guillaume comte d'Eu et d'Hiémois, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Lasseline De Harcourt, tinha 35 anos. Ele casou-se com Mathilde de Sicily aproximadamente 1077, em Catania, Sicilia. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1066. Ele faleceu em 8 de setembro de 1093, em Hastings, Sussex, Inglaterra, com 83 anos, e foi sepultado em Le Tréport, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert d'Eu Lord of Hastings Comte d'Eu родился в 1010 г. в местности эу, сине-маритим, норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Guillaume comte d'Eu et d'Hiémois было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Lasseline De Harcourt, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Mathilde de Sicily около 1077 г. в местности катаня, сисиля. В 1066 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер 8 сентября 1093 г. в местности хастингс, суссекс, Англия в возрасте 83 лет и был похоронен в местности л трeпорт, сине-маритим, норманди, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert d'Eu Lord of Hastings Comte d'Eu nació en 1010, en Eu, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia, su papá, Guillaume comte d'Eu et d'Hiémois, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Lasseline De Harcourt, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Mathilde de Sicily aproximadamente 1077, en Catania, Sicilia. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1066. Murió el 8 de septiembre de 1093, en Hastings, Sussex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 83 años, y fue sepultado en Le Tréport, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia.
